surgeries.name: General Surgery
surgeries.description: Standard preparation applicable to most surgical procedures

weeks_before.task: Get all required blood tests and imaging done
Inform surgeon of all medications and supplements
Arrange for help at home during recovery

days_before.task: Confirm surgery date, time, and location
Review and sign consent forms
Stop blood thinners as directed by surgeon
Stop herbal supplements (may affect bleeding)
Prepare loose, comfortable clothing for hospital
Remove nail polish (for oxygen monitoring)
Pack hospital bag with essentials

night_before.task: Do not eat or drink after midnight (or as directed)
Take only approved medications with small sip of water
Shower with antibacterial soap if instructed
Get a good night's sleep
Set alarm with extra time for arrival

day_of.task: Arrive at hospital at specified time
Bring ID, insurance cards, and advance directive
Remove jewelry, piercings, and contacts
Wear comfortable, loose-fitting clothes
Leave valuables at home
Inform nurse of any last-minute health changes

hospital.task: Follow nurse instructions for getting out of bed
Use pain medication as prescribed
Practice deep breathing exercises
Start walking as soon as allowed
Report severe pain, fever, or unusual symptoms
Understand discharge instructions before leaving

first_week.task: Keep surgical site clean and dry
Watch for signs of infection (redness, swelling, fever)
Avoid driving while on pain medication

warning_signs: Increasing pain not relieved by medication
Redness, swelling, or drainage from incision
Blood clots in legs (pain, swelling in calf)
Inability to urinate or severe constipation

surgeries.name: Heart Surgery
surgeries.description: Preparation for cardiac procedures including bypass, valve replacement

weeks_before.task: Get dental clearance (infections can affect heart)

days_before.task: Stop aspirin/blood thinners as directed
Take prescribed heart medications unless told otherwise
Shower with chlorhexidine soap
Arrange ICU visitation schedule with family
Pack CPAP machine if you use one

night_before.task: No food after midnight
Shower again with antibacterial soap
Sleep in clean sheets
Take approved medications with sip of water in morning

day_of.task: Arrive 2-3 hours before scheduled surgery
Remove dentures and hearing aids before OR
Family should plan to wait 4-8 hours

hospital.task: Expect 1-2 days in ICU with monitoring
Breathing tube will be removed when ready
Start walking within 1-2 days
Do coughing and deep breathing exercises
Hug pillow when coughing to protect sternum
Monitor fluid intake and output

first_week.task: Take all heart medications as prescribed
Check incision daily for signs of infection
Weigh yourself daily (report gain of 2+ lbs)
Sleep on back or side (not stomach)

followup.task: Report any chest pain or shortness of breath

warning_signs: Redness, drainage, or opening of incision

surgeries.name: Joint Replacement
surgeries.description: Hip, knee, or shoulder replacement surgery preparation

weeks_before.task: Start pre-hab exercises to strengthen muscles
Get dental work done before surgery
Install grab bars and raised toilet seat at home
Donate blood if autologous transfusion planned

days_before.task: Stop anti-inflammatory medications
Stop blood thinners as directed
Prepare recovery area on main floor
Stock up on easy-to-prepare foods
Arrange for pet care if needed

night_before.task: Use antibacterial soap/wipes on surgical leg/arm
No food after midnight
Do not shave the surgical area

day_of.task: Wear loose shorts or pants (for hip/knee)
Bring walker/crutches to hospital
Bring list of all current medications

hospital.task: Start physical therapy day of or after surgery
Use CPM machine if prescribed (knee)
Keep leg elevated when sitting
Use blood clot prevention measures
Practice transfers and walking with PT

first_week.task: Use ice for swelling (20 min on, 20 off)
No bending hip past 90 degrees (hip replacement)

followup.task: Inform dentists about joint replacement for antibiotics

warning_signs: Calf pain, redness, or swelling (blood clot)
Joint feels unstable or gives way

surgeries.name: Abdominal Surgery
surgeries.description: Laparoscopic or open abdominal procedures (gallbladder, hernia, etc.)

weeks_before.task: Lose weight if recommended by surgeon
Control diabetes with optimal blood sugars

days_before.task: Follow special diet if instructed (low residue)
Take bowel prep solution if ordered
Stop blood thinners as directed
Buy loose, comfortable clothing

night_before.task: Complete bowel prep (if prescribed)
No solid food after midnight
Clear liquids only until cutoff time

day_of.task: Wear loose pants with elastic waist
Empty bladder before leaving for hospital

hospital.task: Walk as soon as cleared by surgeon
Start with clear liquids, advance diet slowly
Use incentive spirometer for lung health
Report nausea or vomiting

first_week.task: Take stool softeners to prevent constipation

followup.task: Report persistent nausea or bowel changes

warning_signs: Vomiting or inability to keep fluids down

surgeries.name: Eye Surgery
surgeries.description: Cataract, LASIK, or other ophthalmic procedures

weeks_before.task: Stop wearing contact lenses (1-4 weeks for LASIK)
Get eye measurements and tests done
Fill antibiotic and anti-inflammatory eye drops

days_before.task: Do not wear eye makeup for 1-2 days before
Wash eyelids with baby shampoo if instructed
Arrange for someone to stay with you

night_before.task: Get good rest
Do not eat heavy meal morning of surgery
Wash face but avoid eye area

day_of.task: Wear comfortable, loose clothing
Do not wear makeup, lotions, or perfumes
Bring sunglasses for after procedure
Take medications as directed

hospital.task: Rest in recovery until cleared to leave
Wear eye shield as instructed
Have someone drive you home

first_week.task: Use eye drops exactly as prescribed
Do not rub or touch the eye
Avoid getting water in eye (no swimming)

followup.task: Report vision changes or increased pain
Complete full course of eye drops

warning_signs: Flashes of light or new floaters

surgeries.name: Weight Loss Surgery
surgeries.description: Gastric bypass, sleeve gastrectomy, or lap band surgery

weeks_before.task: Stop smoking at least 6 weeks before
Begin vitamin and protein supplement regimen
Practice eating slowly and chewing thoroughly
Start exercise program approved by surgeon

days_before.task: Continue liquid diet strictly
Stop blood thinners as directed
Stay hydrated
Prepare post-op liquid foods at home

night_before.task: Nothing by mouth after midnight
Shower with antibacterial soap

day_of.task: Bring CPAP if you have sleep apnea
Wear loose, comfortable clothing

hospital.task: Walk frequently to prevent blood clots
Use incentive spirometer regularly
Sip small amounts of clear liquids
Report any nausea or pain immediately

first_week.task: Take small sips (1-2 oz at a time)

followup.task: Get regular blood work for vitamin levels
Track food intake and protein goals

warning_signs: Persistent vomiting or inability to keep liquids down

general_tips: Always follow your surgeon's specific instructions over general guidelines
Ask questions if you don't understand something
Have a family member or friend present for discharge instructions
Keep all post-op appointment cards in one place
Write down any questions before appointments
Take photos of your incision to track healing
